issues_found: Source is a Treasury Department hub/listing page that aggregates links to individual documents rather than a standalone article
No specific current debt limit level, X-date, or Treasury cash position provided in source body
Published_at field is empty because no single publication date applies to the hub page
Event_type 'market_notice' is questionable - the actual market notices would be the individual letters to Congress (e.g., Bessent letter 03/14/2025, Yellen letters), not this general hub page
Historical statistics (78 actions since 1960) are presented without specific dates to assess currency
Source contains no specific policy decision, data release, or substantive content beyond general background and a list of links
Core Rule #5 explicitly requires rejection when PAGE_TYPE is navigation_page, listing_page, or homepage
human_review_reason: Source is a generic hub page that aggregates Treasury debt limit correspondence without containing standalone substantive content. It fails the source_type requirement (listing_page) per Rule #5. The linked individual letters to Congress (e.g., Bessent 03/14/2025, Yellen various dates) would be appropriate archive candidates, not this index page.

corrected_fields.summary: This Treasury Department hub page provides general background on the U.S. debt limit mechanism and aggregates links to historical Treasury correspondence. It explains the debt limit authorizes borrowing for existing legal obligations and warns of catastrophic consequences of default. The page is an index of links to individual documents from 2015-2025, not a standalone policy document.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This hub page itself contains no specific current figures or policy decisions. It serves only as a navigational index to individual Treasury letters and reports. Market participants should reference the specific linked documents (Bessent letter 03/14/2025, various Yellen letters, extraordinary measures descriptions) for actionable debt limit intelligence rather than this general archive page.
